translation: H.1 Trace Information for the Motivation Section We present two motivating traces using Qwen2.5 models (1.5B–72B). In the heavy-dominant phase (H), small models(1.5B/3B/7B)servelowbatchsizes(64/64/64)with256-tokenprefill
info_points: H.1 Trace Information for the Motivation Section We present two motivating traces using Qwen2.5 models (1.5B–72B).
source_text: H.1 Trace Information for the Motivation Section We present two motivating traces using Qwen2.5 models (1.5B–72B). In the heavy-dominant phase (H), small models(1.5B/3B/7B)servelowbatchsizes(64/64/64)with256-tokenprefillsequencelengthandshort decode sequence lengths (2048/1536/3072 tokens),while large models (14B/32B/72B) carry high batch loads(384/256/128)with512-tokenprefilllengthandlongdecodesequencelengths(8192/6144/5120 tokens);inthelight-dominantphase(L),smallmodelsdominate(960/480/288batch,256-tokenprefill sequence length, 4096/3072/6144-token decoding sequence length), while large models are minimally loaded (64/32/16 batch, 256-token prefill sequence length, 2048/1536/1280-token decoding sequence length).

source_text: TraceforFigure2(left).AsshowninTable8,this3-timestamptracefeaturestwoabruptphasetransitions, demonstrating the scheduler’s need for rapid response.The system starts in a heavy-dominant phase (ts0), abruptlyshiftstoalight-dominantphase(ts1),andrevertstoheavy-dominantatts2.Eachtransition dramatically redistributes load across all model sizes.

translation: Trace for Figure 2 (right).As shown in Table 9, this 5-timestamp trace hybridizes stable and volatile behavior. The workload begins in a light-dominant phase with minor perturbations (ts0–ts1), undergoes one abrupt shift
info_points: Trace for Figure 2 (right).As shown in Table 9, this 5-timestamp trace hybridizes stable and volatile behavior.
source_text: Trace for Figure 2 (right).As shown in Table 9, this 5-timestamp trace hybridizes stable and volatile behavior. The workload begins in a light-dominant phase with minor perturbations (ts0–ts1), undergoes one abrupt shift to the heavy-dominant phase at ts2, and then stabilizes with minor perturbations (ts3–ts4).This demonstrates that even predominantly stable workloads benefit from adaptive rescheduling at shift boundaries.

translation: H.2 Trace Information for the Case Studies Stable workload in §8.1.We consider a production workload consisting of 10 timestamps (ts0–ts9) and three Qwen2.5 model sizes (1.5B, 3B, and 7B), where batch sizes and sequence
info_points: H.2 Trace Information for the Case Studies Stable workload in §8.1.We consider a production workload consisting of 10 timestamps (ts0–ts9) and three Qwen2.5 model sizes (1.5B, 3B, and 7B), where batch sizes and sequence lengths remain mostly stable with occasional variations.The prefill length is fi
source_text: H.2 Trace Information for the Case Studies Stable workload in §8.1.We consider a production workload consisting of 10 timestamps (ts0–ts9) and three Qwen2.5 model sizes (1.5B, 3B, and 7B), where batch sizes and sequence lengths remain mostly stable with occasional variations.The prefill length is fixed at 256 tokens for all models throughout.The decoding lengths are 4096, 3072, and 6144 tokens for the 1.5B, 3B, and 7B models, respectively.The slight variation exmpales include:at ts1 and ts8, the 1.5B batch increases by 5% (960→1008); at ts2, the 7B batch slightly decreases (288→264); at ts3, the 1.5B decode length doubles to 8192 tokens (batch unchanged); at ts4, the 3B batch increases by 13% (480→544); at ts6, the 7B batch increases by 17% (288→336) and its prefill length doubles to 512 tokens.More detailed batch size variations are shown in Table 10.

info_points: Volatileworkloadin§8.1.Weusea highlydynamicworkload consistingof10timestamps (ts0–ts9)andsix Qwen2.5 model sizes (1.5B–72B), where the system alternates between two contrasting traffic regimes:a heavy-dominant phase and a light-dominant phase.In the heavy phase, small models (1.5B/3B/7B) serve low b
source_text: Volatileworkloadin§8.1.Weusea highlydynamicworkload consistingof10timestamps (ts0–ts9)andsix Qwen2.5 model sizes (1.5B–72B), where the system alternates between two contrasting traffic regimes:a heavy-dominant phase and a light-dominant phase.In the heavy phase, small models (1.5B/3B/7B) serve low batch sizes (64/64/64) with 256-token prefill length and short decode lengths (2048/1536/3072 tokens), while large models (14B/32B/72B) carry high batch loads (384/256/128) with 512-token prefill sequence length and long decode sequence lengths (8192/6144/5120 tokens).In the light phase,the roles reverse: small models dominate with large batches (960/480/288), 256-token prefill sequence length, and decode
